heading: Support for Coast Guard Academy
label: 953 Support for Coast Guard Academy
notes: References in TextSubsection (c)(3) of section 501 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, referred to in subsec. (h)(1), is classified to , Internal Revenue Code.section 501(c)(3) of Title 26

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: The Commandant may enter contract and cooperative agreements with 1 or more qualified organizations for the purpose of supporting the athletic programs of the Coast Guard Academy.

children.heading: .—Authority
children.label: B .—Authority
children.text: Notwithstanding , the Commandant may enter into such contracts and cooperative agreements on a sole source basis pursuant to .section 3201(e) of title 10section 3204(a) of title 10

children.heading: .—Acquisitions
children.label: C .—Acquisitions
children.text: Notwithstanding chapter 63 of title 31, a cooperative agreement under this section may be used to acquire property or services for the direct benefit or use of the Coast Guard Academy.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Before entering into a contract or cooperative agreement under paragraph (1), the Commandant shall ensure that the contract or agreement includes appropriate financial controls to account for the resources of the Coast Guard Academy and the qualified organization concerned in accordance with accepted accounting principles.

children.label: i
children.text: are consistent with the terms of the contract or cooperative agreement; and

children.label: ii
children.text: would compromise the integrity or appearance of integrity of any program of the Department of Homeland Security.

children.heading: .—Leases
children.label: 3 .—Leases
children.text: For the purpose of supporting the athletic programs of the Coast Guard Academy, the Commandant may, consistent with section 504(a)(13), rent or lease real property located at the Coast Guard Academy to a qualified organization, except that proceeds from such a lease shall be retained and expended in accordance with subsection (f).

children.heading: .—Authority
children.label: 1 .—Authority
children.text: To the extent required by a contract or cooperative agreement under subsection (a), the Commandant may provide support services to a qualified organization while the qualified organization conducts support activities at the Coast Guard Academy only if the Commandant determines that the provision of such services is essential for the support of the athletic programs of the Coast Guard Academy.

children.heading: .—No liability of the united states
children.label: 2 .—No liability of the united states
children.text: Support services may only be provided without any liability of the United States to a qualified organization.

children.heading: .—Support services defined
children.label: 3 .—Support services defined
children.text: In this subsection, the term “support services” includes utilities, office furnishings and equipment, communications services, records staging and archiving, audio and video support, and security systems, in conjunction with the leasing or licensing of property.

children.heading: Transfers From Nonappropriated Fund Operation.—
children.label: c Transfers From Nonappropriated Fund Operation.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), the Commandant may, subject to the acceptance of the qualified organization concerned, transfer to the qualified organization all title to and ownership of the assets and liabilities of the Coast Guard nonappropriated fund instrumentality, the function of which includes providing support for the athletic programs of the Coast Guard Academy, including bank accounts and financial reserves in the accounts of such fund instrumentality, equipment, supplies, and other personal property.

children.heading: .—Limitation
children.label: 2 .—Limitation
children.text: The Commandant may not transfer under paragraph (1) any interest in real property.

children.heading: Acceptance of Support From Qualified Organization.—
children.label: d Acceptance of Support From Qualified Organization.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Notwithstanding , the Commandant may accept from a qualified organization funds, supplies, and services for the support of the athletic programs of the Coast Guard Academy.section 1342 of title 31

children.heading: .—Employees of qualified organization
children.label: 2 .—Employees of qualified organization
children.text: For purposes of this section, employees or personnel of the qualified organization may not be considered to be employees of the United States.

children.heading: .—Funds received from ncaa
children.label: 3 .—Funds received from ncaa
children.text: The Commandant may accept funds from the National Collegiate Athletic Association to support the athletic programs of the Coast Guard Academy.

children.label: A
children.text: reflect unfavorably on the ability of the Coast Guard, any employee of the Coast Guard, or any member of the armed forces (as such term is defined in ) to carry out any responsibility or duty in a fair and objective manner; orsection 101(a) of title 10

children.label: B
children.text: compromise the integrity or appearance of integrity of any program of the Coast Guard, or any individual involved in such a program.

children.heading: .—Licensing, marketing, and sponsorship agreements
children.label: 1 .—Licensing, marketing, and sponsorship agreements
children.text: An agreement under subsection (a) may, consistent with (other than subsection (d) of such section), authorize a qualified organization to enter into licensing, marketing, and sponsorship agreements relating to trademarks and service marks identifying the Coast Guard Academy, subject to the approval of the Commandant.section 2260 of title 10

children.label: A
children.text: such agreement would reflect unfavorably on the ability of the Coast Guard, any employee of the Coast Guard, or any member of the armed forces to carry out any responsibility or duty in a fair and objective manner; or

children.label: B
children.text: the Commandant determines that the use of the trademark or service mark would compromise the integrity or appearance of integrity of any program of the Coast Guard or any individual involved in such a program.

children.heading: .—Retention and Use of Funds
children.label: f .—Retention and Use of Funds
children.text: Funds received by the Commandant under this section may be retained for use to support the athletic programs of the Coast Guard Academy and shall remain available until expended.
to operate in accordance with this section, the law of the State of Connecticut, and the constitution and bylaws of the qualified organization; and
to operate exclusively to support the athletic programs of the Coast Guard Academy.
that operates as an organization under subsection (c)(3) of section 501 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 and exempt from taxation under subsection (a) of that section;
for which authorization under sections 1033(a) and 1589(a) of title 10 may be provided; and
established by the Coast Guard Academy Alumni Association solely for the purpose of supporting Coast Guard athletics.
